key_themes: Consistent long-term sales growth (22nd consecutive year expected)
Strong Q3 profit growth (+21% net income) and shareholder returns (raised dividend + major buyback)
Japan aging population tailwinds for nursing/medical care IT services
Short-term lags in Career and Overseas segments with active recovery measures
Neutral analyst consensus but strong YTD stock outperformance
summary: SMS Co., Ltd. (2175.T), a Japanese provider of information infrastructure for nursing, medical, and healthcare services, delivered solid Q3 FY2026 results with sales up 5%, operating income up 9%, and net income up 21% year-over-year, supported by a one-time tax benefit. Results tracked slightly below plan due to slower recovery in certain segments, but the company is executing recovery measures, strategic investments, and expects another year of sales growth amid favorable demographics. It raised its dividend, completed a large buyback (total payout ratio ~91%), and the stock has outperformed the Nikkei YTD (+32%) while trading near analyst price targets with a neutral consensus.[[1]](https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/2175.T/)[[2]](https://global.bm-sms.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/FY25_Q3_E_presentaiton.pdf)[[3]](https://global.bm-sms.com/ir-news/20260130/)
sources: Q3 FY03/2026 earnings release and investor presentation (Jan 2026), Dividend forecast revision and buyback announcement (Jan 30, 2026), Yahoo Finance, Reuters, MarketScreener, company IR site (multiple articles), Analyst consensus (5 analysts: 2 Buy, 2 Hold, 1 Sell; avg target ¥1,748), X/Twitter searches (low volume; <5 directly relevant recent investor posts found; discussion limited in English social media)
